---
schema: verdi.deviation/v1
covers: 024b2458f86204bcd7f3e792893cb38b8bc2612d
findings:
  - { id: judged-ac1-canonical-rerender-not-byte-carry, kind: judged, text: "ac-1 promises every OTHER finding and the digest:/integrity:/judge_integrity: fields are 'carried over byte-for-byte', but runDisposition (cmd/verdi/disposition.go) re-renders the ENTIRE frontmatter via align.RenderMarkdown/renderFrontmatter. For any report align itself wrote this round-trips byte-identically, but for a non-canonically formatted report — exactly the legacy D6-25 hand-edited reports this verb exists to replace — decode-then-re-render silently normalizes every other finding's bytes (quoting, spacing, field order) with no verdict or disclosure. The values survive (VerifyDigest/VerifyIntegrity still pass, which is ac-1's real intent), but the guarantee is value-identity plus canonical normalization, not byte carry-over; notably the BODY path fails closed on any drift (the replaceWholeLine found!=1 verdict) while the frontmatter path silently rewrites — an asymmetry in strictness the spec does not sanction and every test avoids by building fixtures exclusively through the real renderer. (confidence 0.45)", disposition: accepted-deviation, note: "Standing class per ADJ-50 (restated ADJ-52/53/54): value-preserving canonical re-rendering is the store's own determinism discipline, not a departure from it; ac-1's operative verifier claims (digest recompute, VerifyIntegrity) hold unconditionally. Per ADJ-59, this re-records the standing adjudication on the regenerated text." }
  - { id: judged-ac2-verdict-taxonomy-extended-beyond-frozen-spec, kind: judged, text: "ac-2/dc-3 enumerate exactly three exit-1 verdicts and say operational failures are 'distinct from the three verdicts above', but the shipped verb has a fourth exit-1 verdict (body/frontmatter drift for the target finding, per ADJ-53's j-5 reclassification) and a new exit-2 operational class (concurrent-modification staleness refusal, per ADJ-54). Both are defensible and comment-attributed to adjudications, but the frozen spec text (frozen: 2026-07-16) was never revised to name them, so the spec's closed enumeration no longer describes the verb's actual refusal surface — a reader of ac-2 alone would expect a hand-drifted body to be either impossible or operational, not a fourth named verdict. (confidence 0.30)", disposition: accepted-deviation, note: "Standing class per ADJ-53 (j-5 exit-1 reclassification) and ADJ-54 (j-6/j-7 atomic write and staleness check, the durable-writer checklist): both additions to the verdict/exit taxonomy beyond the frozen spec's literal three-verdict enumeration were adjudicated and built deliberately; the frozen spec text is immutable outside ratification, so the gap between its literal enumeration and the adjudicated behavior is the accepted, disclosed divergence this note records. Per ADJ-59, this re-records the standing adjudication on the regenerated text." }
  - { id: judged-rationale-control-char-restriction-unspecced, kind: judged, text: "parseDispositionArgs refuses any --rationale containing a Unicode control character (exit 2, ADJ-52 j-3), a vocabulary restriction no ac/dc/co in the frozen spec states. The schema's note: field can represent such content (YAMLDoubleQuote escapes it in frontmatter), so the verb cannot write or --amend a note the artifact format itself admits — including amending away a legacy hand-edited note that already contains one (its OLD rendered line would also fail the whole-line body match, landing in the drift verdict). The restriction serves ac-1's body-agreement invariant and is adjudicated, but it narrows the verb's input domain below what the spec and schema declare. (confidence 0.25)", disposition: accepted-deviation, note: "Standing class per ADJ-52 (j-3): the control-character rationale refusal was adjudicated and built as ac-1's own body-agreement invariant; the frozen spec's silence on this narrower input domain is the accepted, disclosed consequence of that fix, not a new defect. Per ADJ-59, this re-records the standing adjudication on the regenerated text." }
  - { id: judged-staleness-comment-overclaims-race-closure, kind: judged, text: "disposition.go's package comment and inline ADJ-54 comment claim the optimistic pre-write re-read 'closes the unlocked read-modify-write's lost-update race'; it only narrows it — a concurrent writer landing between the re-read (os.ReadFile) and atomicfile.Write's rename is still silently clobbered. Under this project's three-valued-honesty discipline the honest claim is 'narrows the window to microseconds', not 'closes'; the behavior itself is a reasonable adjudicated trade-off, but the stated guarantee overclaims what the mechanism proves. (confidence 0.20)", disposition: accepted-deviation, note: "Standing class per ADJ-54 (j-7): the optimistic staleness re-read narrows, rather than closes, the lost-update race window; a reasonable adjudicated trade-off whose code comment overstates the guarantee, a low-confidence non-behavioral wording nit (mirroring the ADJ-47/57 comment-accuracy pattern) filed for a future pass rather than a code change under this no-new-code ritual. Per ADJ-59, this re-records the standing adjudication on the regenerated text." }
digest: sha256:a981d8f1238852867830eddcd51cd951f23baeaad91629ba9223c372ae5514da
integrity: sha256:567688ed153f9d8e51df5b311b53466937ad5362d9c6976338bdd985f51c37fa
judge_integrity: { stdin_b64: , raw_result: "```json\n{\"findings\":[{\"id\":\"ac1-canonical-rerender-not-byte-carry\",\"text\":\"ac-1 promises every OTHER finding and the digest:/integrity:/judge_integrity: fields are 'carried over byte-for-byte', but runDisposition (cmd/verdi/disposition.go) re-renders the ENTIRE frontmatter via align.RenderMarkdown/renderFrontmatter. For any report align itself wrote this round-trips byte-identically, but for a non-canonically formatted report — exactly the legacy D6-25 hand-edited reports this verb exists to replace — decode-then-re-render silently normalizes every other finding's bytes (quoting, spacing, field order) with no verdict or disclosure. The values survive (VerifyDigest/VerifyIntegrity still pass, which is ac-1's real intent), but the guarantee is value-identity plus canonical normalization, not byte carry-over; notably the BODY path fails closed on any drift (the replaceWholeLine found!=1 verdict) while the frontmatter path silently rewrites — an asymmetry in strictness the spec does not sanction and every test avoids by building fixtures exclusively through the real renderer.\",\"confidence\":0.45},{\"id\":\"ac2-verdict-taxonomy-extended-beyond-frozen-spec\",\"text\":\"ac-2/dc-3 enumerate exactly three exit-1 verdicts and say operational failures are 'distinct from the three verdicts above', but the shipped verb has a fourth exit-1 verdict (body/frontmatter drift for the target finding, per ADJ-53's j-5 reclassification) and a new exit-2 operational class (concurrent-modification staleness refusal, per ADJ-54). Both are defensible and comment-attributed to adjudications, but the frozen spec text (frozen: 2026-07-16) was never revised to name them, so the spec's closed enumeration no longer describes the verb's actual refusal surface — a reader of ac-2 alone would expect a hand-drifted body to be either impossible or operational, not a fourth named verdict.\",\"confidence\":0.3},{\"id\":\"rationale-control-char-restriction-unspecced\",\"text\":\"parseDispositionArgs refuses any --rationale containing a Unicode control character (exit 2, ADJ-52 j-3), a vocabulary restriction no ac/dc/co in the frozen spec states. The schema's note: field can represent such content (YAMLDoubleQuote escapes it in frontmatter), so the verb cannot write or --amend a note the artifact format itself admits — including amending away a legacy hand-edited note that already contains one (its OLD rendered line would also fail the whole-line body match, landing in the drift verdict). The restriction serves ac-1's body-agreement invariant and is adjudicated, but it narrows the verb's input domain below what the spec and schema declare.\",\"confidence\":0.25},{\"id\":\"staleness-comment-overclaims-race-closure\",\"text\":\"disposition.go's package comment and inline ADJ-54 comment claim the optimistic pre-write re-read 'closes the unlocked read-modify-write's lost-update race'; it only narrows it — a concurrent writer landing between the re-read (os.ReadFile) and atomicfile.Write's rename is still silently clobbered. Under this project's three-valued-honesty discipline the honest claim is 'narrows the window to microseconds', not 'closes'; the behavior itself is a reasonable adjudicated trade-off, but the stated guarantee overclaims what the mechanism proves.\",\"confidence\":0.2}]}\n```" }
frozen: { at: 2026-07-17, commit: 024b2458f86204bcd7f3e792893cb38b8bc2612d }
provenance: { generator: verdi-align, version: v0, inputs: [spec/disposition-verb@024b2458f86204bcd7f3e792893cb38b8bc2612d, spec/disposition-verb@d2ecf50f0e6f8a3163692abce22fe55de7adf3c2], digest: sha256:a981d8f1238852867830eddcd51cd951f23baeaad91629ba9223c372ae5514da, integrity: sha256:567688ed153f9d8e51df5b311b53466937ad5362d9c6976338bdd985f51c37fa }
---
# Alignment report

## Computed

(none)

### Boundary diff vs acceptance baseline

(no impacted services)

### Diagram alignment

- (no accepted proposals)
- (no illustrative diagrams in this spec's body)

## Judged

- **judged-ac1-canonical-rerender-not-byte-carry** [accepted-deviation]: ac-1 promises every OTHER finding and the digest:/integrity:/judge_integrity: fields are 'carried over byte-for-byte', but runDisposition (cmd/verdi/disposition.go) re-renders the ENTIRE frontmatter via align.RenderMarkdown/renderFrontmatter. For any report align itself wrote this round-trips byte-identically, but for a non-canonically formatted report — exactly the legacy D6-25 hand-edited reports this verb exists to replace — decode-then-re-render silently normalizes every other finding's bytes (quoting, spacing, field order) with no verdict or disclosure. The values survive (VerifyDigest/VerifyIntegrity still pass, which is ac-1's real intent), but the guarantee is value-identity plus canonical normalization, not byte carry-over; notably the BODY path fails closed on any drift (the replaceWholeLine found!=1 verdict) while the frontmatter path silently rewrites — an asymmetry in strictness the spec does not sanction and every test avoids by building fixtures exclusively through the real renderer. (confidence 0.45) — Standing class per ADJ-50 (restated ADJ-52/53/54): value-preserving canonical re-rendering is the store's own determinism discipline, not a departure from it; ac-1's operative verifier claims (digest recompute, VerifyIntegrity) hold unconditionally. Per ADJ-59, this re-records the standing adjudication on the regenerated text.
- **judged-ac2-verdict-taxonomy-extended-beyond-frozen-spec** [accepted-deviation]: ac-2/dc-3 enumerate exactly three exit-1 verdicts and say operational failures are 'distinct from the three verdicts above', but the shipped verb has a fourth exit-1 verdict (body/frontmatter drift for the target finding, per ADJ-53's j-5 reclassification) and a new exit-2 operational class (concurrent-modification staleness refusal, per ADJ-54). Both are defensible and comment-attributed to adjudications, but the frozen spec text (frozen: 2026-07-16) was never revised to name them, so the spec's closed enumeration no longer describes the verb's actual refusal surface — a reader of ac-2 alone would expect a hand-drifted body to be either impossible or operational, not a fourth named verdict. (confidence 0.30) — Standing class per ADJ-53 (j-5 exit-1 reclassification) and ADJ-54 (j-6/j-7 atomic write and staleness check, the durable-writer checklist): both additions to the verdict/exit taxonomy beyond the frozen spec's literal three-verdict enumeration were adjudicated and built deliberately; the frozen spec text is immutable outside ratification, so the gap between its literal enumeration and the adjudicated behavior is the accepted, disclosed divergence this note records. Per ADJ-59, this re-records the standing adjudication on the regenerated text.
- **judged-rationale-control-char-restriction-unspecced** [accepted-deviation]: parseDispositionArgs refuses any --rationale containing a Unicode control character (exit 2, ADJ-52 j-3), a vocabulary restriction no ac/dc/co in the frozen spec states. The schema's note: field can represent such content (YAMLDoubleQuote escapes it in frontmatter), so the verb cannot write or --amend a note the artifact format itself admits — including amending away a legacy hand-edited note that already contains one (its OLD rendered line would also fail the whole-line body match, landing in the drift verdict). The restriction serves ac-1's body-agreement invariant and is adjudicated, but it narrows the verb's input domain below what the spec and schema declare. (confidence 0.25) — Standing class per ADJ-52 (j-3): the control-character rationale refusal was adjudicated and built as ac-1's own body-agreement invariant; the frozen spec's silence on this narrower input domain is the accepted, disclosed consequence of that fix, not a new defect. Per ADJ-59, this re-records the standing adjudication on the regenerated text.
- **judged-staleness-comment-overclaims-race-closure** [accepted-deviation]: disposition.go's package comment and inline ADJ-54 comment claim the optimistic pre-write re-read 'closes the unlocked read-modify-write's lost-update race'; it only narrows it — a concurrent writer landing between the re-read (os.ReadFile) and atomicfile.Write's rename is still silently clobbered. Under this project's three-valued-honesty discipline the honest claim is 'narrows the window to microseconds', not 'closes'; the behavior itself is a reasonable adjudicated trade-off, but the stated guarantee overclaims what the mechanism proves. (confidence 0.20) — Standing class per ADJ-54 (j-7): the optimistic staleness re-read narrows, rather than closes, the lost-update race window; a reasonable adjudicated trade-off whose code comment overstates the guarantee, a low-confidence non-behavioral wording nit (mirroring the ADJ-47/57 comment-accuracy pattern) filed for a future pass rather than a code change under this no-new-code ritual. Per ADJ-59, this re-records the standing adjudication on the regenerated text.
